case_number: constrained to observe that no case

chunks.text: IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH CIRCUIT COURT, LARKANA Present: Irshad Ali Shah, J. Agha Faisal, J. CP D308of 2019: Muhib Hussain Pirzadovs. Federation of Pakistan &Others For the Petitioners: Mr. Irshad Ali R. Chandio, Advocate For the Respondent 1: Mr. Muhammad Imran Abbasi, Assistant Attorney General For the Respondent 2: Mr. Abdul Hamid Bhurgri, Additional Advocate General. For the Respondent 3: Mr. Bashir Ahmed Dargahi, Advocate Dates of hearing: 22.02.2022 Date of announcement: 22.02.2022 ORDER Agha Faisal, J. The petitioner, demonstrably devoid of locus standi, seeks issuance of a writ against S.S.G.C, being a private respondent, for installation of gas infrastructure in five identified villages. Learned counsel has been unable to substantiate as to how the petitioner was aggrieved and further as to how a writ could be issued to a private respondent. No reason was articulated as to the basis for the claim for the cited region to be given precedence in terms of supply of gas over other areas / regions. In view hereof, this Court is constrained to observe that no case has been set forth for the invocation of the discretionary writ jurisdiction of this Court, hence, this petition is hereby dismissed. JUDGE JUDGE Per Ijaz Ul Ahsan J. in Syed Iqbal Hussain Shah Gillani vs. PBC & Others reported as 2021 SCMR 425; Muhammad Fiaz Khan vs. Ajmer Khan & Another reported as 2010 SCMR 105.
